title: The transfiguration part 3
scripture_text: “Truly I tell you, some who are standing here will not taste death before they see the Son of Man coming in his kingdom.” After six days Jesus took with him Peter, James and John the brother of James, and led them up a high mountain by themselves. There he was transfigured before them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as the light. Just then there appeared before them Moses and Elijah, talking with Jesus, Peter said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you wish, I will put up three shelters—one for you, one for Moses and one for Elijah.” While he was still speaking, a bright cloud covered them, and a voice from the cloud said,“This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased. Listen to him!’’ When the disciples heard this, they fell face down to the ground, terrified. But Jesus came and touched them. “Get up’’ he said. “Don’t be afraid.” When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus. As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus instructed them, “Don’t tell anyone what you have seen, until the Son of Man has been raised from the dead.’’ The disciples asked him, “Why then do the teachers of the law say that Elijah must come first?” Jesus replied, “To be sure, Elijah comes and will restore all things. But I tell you, Elijah has already come and they did not recognize him, but have done to him everything they wished. In the same way the Son of Man is going to suffer at their hands.” Then the disciples understood that he was talking to them about John the Baptist.
devotional:
In Jewish liturgy there was a daily prayer called the ‘Shema.’ It is the Hebrew word that begins the most important prayer in Judaism. It is found in Deuteronomy 6:4, which begins with the command to “Hear.” The whole Shema prayer, which includes verses 4-9, is spoken daily in the Jewish tradition: The word Shema means to listen.

Look at what Matthew says.

Peter said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you wish, I will put up three shelters—one for you, one for Moses and one for Elijah.” While he was still speaking, a bright cloud covered them, and a voice from the cloud said, “This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased. Listen to him!’’

A voice from heaven, the voice of God speaking directly to the three disciples telling them to listen to his Son. Sometimes we are unable to hear what Jesus is saying to us in the present because we are so accustomed to listening to the voices of the past. We suffer from nostalgic hearing. Although the voices of the past may have been good and important, it is time for a new listen to a new Shema. A new attentiveness to Jesus.

I find it remarkable that as the disciples came down the mountain with Jesus they got into a theological discussion about Elijah. God had just spoken to them from heaven, this is only the second time this had happened in hundreds of years. In the minds of these disciples, Jesus had to be at least equal to Moses an Elijah, but they had not really grasped his full identity. So the Father says ‘This is my Son whom I love, with him I am well pleased, listen to him.’ Here was God the father revealing his heart, talking about what was most important to him, sharing the pleasure he found in Jesus.

As Christians there can be times of uncertainty in our relationship with Jesus. Sometimes we have expectations that are not met, dreams that are dashed and hopes that feel shattered. The Jesus we thought we knew can be different to the Jesus we are experiencing. It is a times like this we need to experience a new intimacy, a new appreciation of his glory, a new realisation that the Jesus we love and worship is greater and bigger than we will ever know or understand.
prayer: Father, help me to hear Jesus above all the other voices in my life.
html:
The transfiguration part 3

Study Text: Matthew 16:28-17:1-13
“Truly I tell you, some who are standing here will not taste death before they see the Son of Man coming in his kingdom.” After six days Jesus took with him Peter, James and John the brother of James, and led them up a high mountain by themselves. There he was transfigured before them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as the light. Just then there appeared before them Moses and Elijah, talking with Jesus, Peter said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you wish, I will put up three shelters—one for you, one for Moses and one for Elijah.” While he was still speaking, a bright cloud covered them, and a voice from the cloud said,“This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased. Listen to him!’’ When the disciples heard this, they fell face down to the ground, terrified. But Jesus came and touched them. “Get up’’ he said. “Don’t be afraid.” When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus. As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus instructed them, “Don’t tell anyone what you have seen, until the Son of Man has been raised from the dead.’’ The disciples asked him, “Why then do the teachers of the law say that Elijah must come first?” Jesus replied, “To be sure, Elijah comes and will restore all things. But I tell you, Elijah has already come and they did not recognize him, but have done to him everything they wished. In the same way the Son of Man is going to suffer at their hands.” Then the disciples understood that he was talking to them about John the Baptist.

In Jewish liturgy there was a daily prayer called the ‘Shema.’ It is the Hebrew word that begins the most important prayer in Judaism. It is found in Deuteronomy 6:4, which begins with the command to “Hear.” The whole Shema prayer, which includes verses 4-9, is spoken daily in the Jewish tradition: The word Shema means to listen.

Look at what Matthew says.

Peter said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you wish, I will put up three shelters—one for you, one for Moses and one for Elijah.” While he was still speaking, a bright cloud covered them, and a voice from the cloud said, “This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased. Listen to him!’’

A voice from heaven, the voice of God speaking directly to the three disciples telling them to listen to his Son. Sometimes we are unable to hear what Jesus is saying to us in the present because we are so accustomed to listening to the voices of the past. We suffer from nostalgic hearing. Although the voices of the past may have been good and important, it is time for a new listen to a new Shema. A new attentiveness to Jesus.

I find it remarkable that as the disciples came down the mountain with Jesus they got into a theological discussion about Elijah. God had just spoken to them from heaven, this is only the second time this had happened in hundreds of years. In the minds of these disciples, Jesus had to be at least equal to Moses an Elijah, but they had not really grasped his full identity. So the Father says ‘This is my Son whom I love, with him I am well pleased, listen to him.’ Here was God the father revealing his heart, talking about what was most important to him, sharing the pleasure he found in Jesus.

As Christians there can be times of uncertainty in our relationship with Jesus. Sometimes we have expectations that are not met, dreams that are dashed and hopes that feel shattered. The Jesus we thought we knew can be different to the Jesus we are experiencing. It is a times like this we need to experience a new intimacy, a new appreciation of his glory, a new realisation that the Jesus we love and worship is greater and bigger than we will ever know or understand.

Prayer: Father, help me to hear Jesus above all the other voices in my life.
Bible in a year reading plan: - 1 Chronicles 11-13
